message:
prelude: promote 11 fp-types pandas-equivalent helpers

Fifth in the paired-surface defect series (fd90.10/11/12/13). The
fd90.263 / fd90.271 commits added pandas-equivalent helper types and
range constructors at the crate root but missed prelude promotion.

Promoted to the prelude:
- Interval / IntervalClosed     (paired with Scalar::Interval)
- Period / PeriodFreq           (paired with Scalar::Period)
- Timedelta / TimedeltaComponents / TimedeltaError
                                (paired with Scalar::Timedelta64)
- Timestamp                     (paired with Scalar::Datetime64)
- period_range                  (pandas pd.period_range)
- interval_range_by_periods     (pandas pd.interval_range periods=)
- interval_range_by_step        (pandas pd.interval_range step=)

Adds fd90_014_fp_types_helpers_via_prelude — compile guard fn naming
each type in its signature plus referencing the three module-level
range helpers, locking in the surface.

Closes frankenpandas-fd90.14.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankenpandas-fd90.11","title":"test(readme): cover module-level SQL helpers (list_sql_*, sql_*) + promote to prelude","description":"Sister to fd90.9/fd90.10. The 11 module-level free-function SQL helpers — paired with SqlInspector methods — are exposed at the crate root but absent from the prelude:

- list_sql_foreign_keys
- list_sql_indexes
- list_sql_schemas
- list_sql_tables
- list_sql_unique_constraints
- list_sql_views
- sql_max_identifier_length
- sql_primary_key_columns
- sql_server_version
- sql_table_comment
- sql_table_schema

Each pairs 1:1 with a SqlInspector method (which IS in the prelude). README line 148 documents the same introspection surface. inspect() had the same defect (fixed in fd90.10) — these are the rest of that surface.

Plan:
- Add module-level coverage to readme_quick_start_round_trip_through_sqlite (call each helper against the parent/child schema and verify against the SqlInspector results from fd90.9/fd90.10)
- Promote the 11 helpers to the prelude (pairs with SqlInspector being there)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T11:37:15.990474738Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T11:39:36.666404169Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T11:39:36.664316304Z","close_reason":"completed: 12 module-level SQL helpers covered + promoted to prelude (commit 8a1961c)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-fd90.11","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-fd90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-27T11:37:15.990474738Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-fd90.12","title":"test(readme): cover Series Arrow interop (series_from_arrow_array / series_to_arrow_array) + promote to prelude","description":"Sister to fd90.10 / fd90.11. The two public Series-level Arrow interop functions are exposed at the crate root (lib.rs lines 206-207) but missing from the prelude AND not exercised by any integration test:

- series_to_arrow_array(series) -> (ArrowDataType, Arc<dyn Array>)
- series_from_arrow_array(name, labels, &arr, &dtype) -> Series

README line 1580 documents Arrow interop as a public surface. fd90.264 added these — they should be in the prelude alongside other paired top-level/prelude surfaces.

Plan:
- Add round-trip integration test: build a Series, convert via series_to_arrow_array, rebuild via series_from_arrow_array, verify identity.
- Promote both functions to the prelude.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T11:42:50.351062864Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T11:45:16.455002725Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T11:45:16.452947853Z","close_reason":"completed: Series ↔ Arrow interop covered with round-trip test + promoted to prelude (commit d93fe0b)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-fd90.12","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-fd90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-27T11:42:50.351062864Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-fd90.13","title":"prelude: promote 9 SQL schema/iterator return types (SqlChunkIterator/Schema/Reflected/etc.)","description":"Fourth in the paired-surface defect series (fd90.10/11/12 caught inspect, list_sql_*, series_*_arrow_array). Found by systematic top-level vs prelude diff.

Nine SQL types are exposed at the crate root but absent from the prelude despite being the public RETURN TYPES of SqlInspector methods (which IS in the prelude). Users calling inspector.columns() get back a SqlTableSchema but can't name the type they got via prelude alone.

Missing from prelude:
- SqlChunkIterator        — return type of read_sql_chunks
- SqlColumnSchema         — element type of SqlTableSchema.columns
- SqlForeignKeySchema     — return type of inspector.foreign_keys
- SqlIndexSchema          — return type of inspector.indexes
- SqlIndexedChunkIterator — chunked iterator with index_col
- SqlQueryResult          — return type of inspector queries
- SqlReflectedTable       — return type of inspector.reflect_table
- SqlTableSchema          — return type of inspector.columns
- SqlUniqueConstraintSchema — return type of inspector.unique_constraints

Plan: promote all 9 to the prelude. Add a compile guard test that names each type explicitly to lock in the surface.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T11:46:52.930655526Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T11:48:33.049110151Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T11:48:33.046939279Z","close_reason":"completed: 9 SQL schema/iterator types promoted to prelude with compile guard (commit 171e285)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-fd90.13","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-fd90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-27T11:46:52.930655526Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-fd90.14","title":"prelude: promote 7 fp-types user-facing helpers (Interval/Period/Timedelta/Timestamp + helpers)","description":"Fifth paired-surface defect (fd90.10-13 fixed inspect, list_sql_*, series_*_arrow_array, SQL schema return types).

The fd90.263 / fd90.271 commits added 7 pandas-equivalent helper types and 3 range constructors at the crate root but missed prelude promotion. Users frequently need these: Scalar variants for Datetime64/Timedelta64/Period/Interval already round-trip through Scalar, but richer parsing / inspection requires the helper types.

Missing from prelude:
- Interval                  (paired with Scalar::Interval; from fd90.263)
- IntervalClosed            (variant param for Interval construction)
- Period                    (paired with Scalar::Period)
- PeriodFreq                (variant param for Period construction)
- Timedelta                 (paired with Scalar::Timedelta64)
- TimedeltaComponents       (struct returned by Timedelta::components())
- TimedeltaError            (error type for Timedelta::parse / arithmetic)
- Timestamp                 (paired with Scalar::Datetime64)
- period_range              (pandas pd.period_range)
- interval_range_by_periods (pandas pd.interval_range)
- interval_range_by_step    (pandas pd.interval_range with step)

Plan: promote all 11 to the prelude with a compile guard.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T11:49:16.333254334Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T11:49:51.212679488Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-fd90.14","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-fd90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-27T11:49:16.333254334Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"frankenpandas-fd90.4","title":"fp-io SQL chunks index_col helpers","description":"Child slice of frankenpandas-fd90: add chunked SQL read helpers that combine pandas-style chunksize with index_col promotion. Cover read_sql/read_sql_query/read_sql_table chunk paths by wrapping SqlChunkIterator, preserving per-chunk RangeIndex when index_col is None and promoting/removing the named column when set. Add umbrella re-exports and focused fp-io tests for query chunks, table chunks, None behavior, and missing index_col errors.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod-pandas","created_at":"2026-04-25T08:00:11.325655162Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T08:08:08.040272870Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T08:08:08.038384696Z","close_reason":"Completed in 90fd549","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["io","parity","sql"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-fd90.4","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-fd90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-25T08:00:11.325655162Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
